_comment: OpenRouter / remote-provider example pool (RDR-012). Shows how to add authenticated remote OpenAI-compatible backends alongside the local fleet. Credentials resolve from env at request time via api_key_env (preferred over a literal api_key — keeps secrets out of any tree the supervisor reads). Set OPENROUTER_API_KEY / TOGETHER_API_KEY in the supervisor's environment. NOTE (RDR-012): the `headers` field (OpenRouter HTTP-Referer/X-Title attribution) is honored on the DIRECT-HTTP tools (qwen_chat / qwen_oneshot_vision / qwen_embed / qwen_rerank / qwen_tokenize) but NOT on the agentic path (qwen_spawn / qwen_oneshot) — the @qwen-code SDK has no header channel. OpenRouter works without them; only dashboard attribution is affected. Remote agentic backends bypass the prompt-size capacity heuristic by design: pin them via opts.backend or route by role, don't expect the size heuristic to pick them.

backends._comment: THE local coder (box, llama.cpp). Unchanged from the vision pool — agentic coding default. No auth (local llama-server). Pooled with the remote agentic backend below by tier=remote/capacity=heavy IF you want weighted round-robin across them; otherwise pin per call.

Remote AGENTIC backend via OpenRouter — a frontier model for the hard tail. RDR-012 lets qwen_spawn/qwen_oneshot carry this backend's api_key. Pin via opts.backend='openrouter-sonnet' for the hard SWE-bench tail, or give it a low weight to pool it with coder-box. capacity=heavy + tier=remote so it sits in the heavy/remote pool. The headers below apply only to direct-HTTP tools (see top comment).

Remote CHAT/operator backend via OpenRouter — direct-HTTP path (qwen_chat role=general). Full header support here (direct-HTTP). A cheap fast model for operator-side synthesis. no_agentic keeps it out of the agentic coding pool.
Remote embeddings via Together (direct-HTTP modality select). Demonstrates a second provider + a literal-key-free env credential. Selected by qwen_embed.
